{{Short description="Electromagnetic radiation with the longest wavelengths}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Radio wave
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Electromagnetic wave]], [[Electromagnetic spectrum]], [[Photon]]
| wavelength = >0.1 m
| frequency = <3 GHz
}}

'''Radio waves''' are a type of [[electromagnetic radiation]] with [[wavelength]]s in the [[electromagnetic spectrum]] longer than [[infrared]] light. They have frequencies from 3 [[kHz]] to 300 [[GHz]] and corresponding wavelengths from 100 km to 1 mm. Radio waves travel at the [[speed of light]] in vacuum and are generated by [[electric charge|charged particles]] undergoing [[acceleration]].

== Properties ==
* Longest wavelengths / lowest frequencies in the EM spectrum
* Low [[photon]] energy: <math>E = h\nu < 10^{-3}</math> eV
* Can diffract around obstacles (especially at lower frequencies)
* Can propagate beyond the horizon via [[ionosphere|ionospheric]] reflection (skywave)
* Penetrate non-conductive materials (walls, foliage)

== Generation ==
Radio waves are produced by time-varying [[electric current]]s in [[antenna (radio)|antennas]]. The oscillating charges create oscillating electric and magnetic fields that radiate away as electromagnetic waves.

== Bands ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Band !! Frequency !! Wavelength !! Applications
|-
| ELF || 3–30 Hz || 10⁵–10⁴ km || Submarine communication
|-
| VLF || 3–30 kHz || 100–10 km || Navigation
|-
| LF || 30–300 kHz || 10–1 km || AM radio (LW)
|-
| MF || 300–3000 kHz || 1 km–100 m || AM radio (MW)
|-
| HF || 3–30 MHz || 100–10 m || Shortwave, amateur radio
|-
| VHF || 30–300 MHz || 10–1 m || FM radio, TV
|-
| UHF || 300–3000 MHz || 1 m–10 cm || TV, mobile phones, Wi-Fi
|}

== Applications ==
* [[Broadcasting]] — AM/FM radio, television
* [[Telecommunications]] — mobile phones, [[Wi-Fi]], [[Bluetooth]], satellite communication
* [[Radar]] — detection and ranging
* [[Radio astronomy]] — observing celestial radio sources (pulsars, cosmic microwave background)
* [[MRI]] — magnetic resonance imaging uses radio frequency pulses

== Propagation ==
* '''Ground wave''' — follows Earth's surface (up to ~2 MHz)
* '''Skywave''' — reflected by the ionosphere (3–30 MHz)
* '''Line of sight''' — direct path (>30 MHz)
* '''Space wave''' — satellite communication

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Electromagnetic spectrum]]
* [[Electromagnetic wave]]
* [[Microwave]]
* [[Photon]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Radio waves| ]]
[[Category:Electromagnetic radiation]]
[[Category:Telecommunications]]
